zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Blanche Hart於大約1420年出生在英國爾達頓時，她的父親Sir Andrew Mathew Hart21歲，母親Caroline  Matilda Welty21歲。她於1442年與William James Hutchison在英國結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Blanche Hart于大约1420年出生在英国爾達頓时，她的父亲Sir Andrew Mathew Hart21岁，母亲Caroline  Matilda Welty21岁。她于1442年与William James Hutchison在英国结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Hart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and North German: nickname from Middle English hert (Old English heorot), Middle Low German hërte, harte ‘hart, stag’, perhaps for a quick-footed or timorous individual.
German: variant of   Hardt   1 and 2. It is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from German and Yiddish hart ‘hard’.

variants.name: Hartmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Manfred, Dieter, Dietrich, Franz.
North German and Danish: variant of   Hart   1.

German and Swiss German: from an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements hard ‘hard, strong’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in the Netherlands, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Hartman .

variants.name: Francoeur
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Jacques, Aime, Marcel, Normand, Alcide, Amie, Anselme, Cecile, Clemence, Dominique, Georges.
French:
soldier's name from franc coeur ‘sincere heart’.

variants.name: Hartin
meanings: Irish (Donegal): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArtáin ‘descendant of Artán’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of Art, a byname meaning ‘bear’ or ‘hero’. Compare Hart 8.
English: habitational name from Harting (Sussex), from the Old English personal name Heort + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas.

variants.name: Hartel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Erna, Gunter, Kurt, Lutz, Otto.
German (also Härtel): from a pet form of an ancient Germanic compound personal name based on the element hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’.
French (Normandy): occupational name, a derivative of   Hart   7.

variants.name: De Hart
meanings: Dutch: from Middle Dutch hart, modern Dutch hert ‘deer, hart’ or hart ‘heart’ (see Hart 5), with the definite article de ‘the’.

variants.name: Hardt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Ernst, Erwin, Frieda, Gerhard, Gottlieb, Gunther, Hans, Heinz, Reinhold, Udo.
German: topographic name for someone who lived by woods used as pasture, from Middle High German hart.
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic compound personal name beginning with hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’.

variants.name: Harten
meanings: North German: patronymic from   Hart   1.
Dutch: patronymic from   Hart   4.
Dutch (Van Harten): habitational name from a place so named near Renkum, Gelderland.

variants.name: Hartt
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Hart  .

variants.name: Harte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sinead.
English and North German: variant of   Hart   1.
Dutch: variant of   Hart   4.

variants.name: Hartjes
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from a pet form of   Hart   4.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: Formed from Margaret.

nameMeanings.name: Blanche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname for a blonde, from blanche, feminine of Old French blanc ‘white’ (of Germanic origin). It came to be associated with the notion of whiteness as indicating purity, and was introduced into England as a given name by the Normans. A pale complexion combined with light hair was long an ideal of beauty in Europe (compare Modern English fair, which at first meant ‘beautiful’ and then, from the 16th century, ‘light in colouring’).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Blanche Hart was born about 1420, in Dutton, Cheshire, England, her father, Sir Andrew Mathew Hart, was 21 and her mother, Caroline  Matilda Welty, was 21. She married William James Hutchison in 1442, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Blanche Hart est née environ 1420, à Dutton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Andrew Mathew Hart, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Caroline  Matilda Welty, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé William James Hutchison en 1442, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Blanche Hart um 1420 in Dutton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Andrew Mathew Hart 21 und ihre Mutter Caroline  Matilda Welty 21. Sie hat 1442 in England William James Hutchison geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Blanche Hart è nata il circa 1420 nel Dutton, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Sir Andrew Mathew Hart, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Caroline  Matilda Welty, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato William James Hutchison nel 1442, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie.

ja.Dutton, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアダットオン
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Blanche Hartが約1420年にイギリスチェシュアイアダットオンで生まれた時，父Sir Andrew Mathew Hartは21歳，母Caroline  Matilda Weltyは21歳であった。1442年にイギリスでWilliam James Hutchisonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Blanche Hart(이)가 대략 1420년 영국 체실르 드턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Andrew Mathew Hart(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Caroline  Matilda Welty(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1442년 영국에서 William James Hutchison(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Blanche Hart nasceu aproximadamente 1420, em Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Andrew Mathew Hart, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Caroline  Matilda Welty, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com William James Hutchison em 1442, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Blanche Hart родилась около 1420 г. в местности дуттон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Andrew Mathew Hart было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Caroline  Matilda Welty, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William James Hutchison в 1442 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Blanche Hart nació aproximadamente 1420, en Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Andrew Mathew Hart, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Caroline  Matilda Welty, tenía 21 años. Se casó con William James Hutchison en 1442, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas.
